body.title: agent_datacenter: bounded per-device inbound message queue with drop policy
body.result: DeviceQueue in agent_datacenter/skeleton/device_queue.py. drop-oldest/newest per DeviceConfig. drain() returns in order + clears. Warning logged on drop. All tests pass.
body.description:
Messages sent to a device mid-restart must not vanish. Each device has a bounded inbound queue that drains in order on restart.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/skeleton/device_queue.py:
  class DeviceQueue:
    def __init__(self, device_id: str, config: DeviceConfig)
    def enqueue(self, envelope: Envelope) -> None:
      if len(queue) >= max_queue_length:
        if drop_newest: discard incoming; log 'Queue full; dropping newest'
        else: queue.popleft(); log 'Queue full; dropping oldest'
      queue.append(envelope)
    def drain(self) -> list[Envelope]: return all queued messages in order; clear queue
    def __len__(self) -> int
  Queue stored in memory (not persisted — small loss window on crash is acceptable per spec)
  Integrated into Skeleton: messages routed to a restarting device go to DeviceQueue; on restart complete, drain() before forwarding new messages

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/device_queue.py (new)
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py (enqueue integration)

Design rules:
- oop-first: DeviceQueue class using collections.deque internally

Scope boundary:
IN: bounded queue, drop-oldest/newest policy, drain on restart
OUT: persistent queue across skeleton restarts (small window loss acceptable per spec)

Test plan:
- Enqueue 101 messages with max_queue_length=100, drop_newest=False → 100 messages; oldest dropped; log written
- Enqueue 101 messages with drop_newest=True → 100 messages; 101st discarded; log written
- drain() returns messages in order, queue empty after
- DeviceQueue(config=DeviceConfig()) instantiates with defaults
